title: podman stop -t -1 container-id issue
body:
### Issue Description

The online [documentation](https://docs.podman.io/en/stable/markdown/podman-stop.1.html) for podman stop command says
```
--time, -t=seconds

Seconds to wait before forcibly stopping the container. Use -1 for
infinite wait.
```
However using -1 as timeout shows overflow or a 64bit unsigned integer and results in an immediate SIGKILL

```
$ podman stop -t -1 indimail-mta
WARN[0000] StopSignal SIGTERM failed to stop container indimail-mta in 18446744073709551615 seconds, resorting to SIGKILL
indimail-mta
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. start any container
2. stop the container using `podman stop -1 container-id`


### Describe the results you received

immediate SIGKILL of the container

### Describe the results you expected

Specifying -1 should result in letting the container stop when all processes stop
